annotations.label: Integral of a form in a single chart

anchor.section: Formulation for smooth manifolds with boundary

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the integral of a differential n-form on a manifold via a single chart.

anchors.section: Formulation for smooth manifolds with boundary

annotations.label: Integral via partition of unity

anchor.section: Formulation for smooth manifolds with boundary

annotations.note: Smooth partitions of unity exist in `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.PartitionOfUnity`, but no integration of differential forms over manifolds via them is defined.

anchors.section: Formulation for smooth manifolds with boundary

annotations.label: Stokes–Cartan theorem
annotations.note: The general Stokes–Cartan identity ∫_M dω = ∫_∂M ω for compact oriented manifolds with boundary is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Singular k-simplex and k-chains

anchor.snippet: A (smooth) singular k -simplex in M is defined as a smooth map from the standard simplex

annotations.note: Mathlib has algebraic-topology simplicial machinery but no notion of smooth singular k-simplex in a manifold or its free-abelian chain group used here.

annotations.label: de Rham cohomology

anchor.snippet: the differential forms, with exterior derivative, d , as the connecting map, form a cochain complex

annotations.note: `extDeriv` exists on normed spaces but the de Rham cochain complex/cohomology is not defined; the only file mentioning de Rham is `BDeRham` in p-adic Hodge theory.

annotations.label: Forms as functionals on chains; chain map property

anchor.snippet: Differential k -forms can be integrated over a k -simplex in a natural way

annotations.note: Integration of forms over smooth chains and the resulting cochain map are not in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Differential k -forms can be integrated over a k -simplex in a natural way

annotations.label: De Rham's theorem

anchor.snippet: De Rham's theorem shows that this homomorphism is in fact an isomorphism

annotations.note: Neither de Rham nor singular cohomology of smooth manifolds is in Mathlib, so the isomorphism between them is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: De Rham's theorem shows that this homomorphism is in fact an isomorphism

annotations.label: Stokes' theorem for chains
annotations.note: The chain-level version ∫_{∂c} ω = ∫_c dω is absent since smooth chains and integration over them are not defined.

annotations.label: Underlying principle in d = 2

anchor.snippet: by considering an example for d = 2 dimensions

annotations.note: The 2-dimensional cancellation argument is captured by the planar divergence theorem on rectangles in `integral2_divergence_prod_of_hasFDerivAt`.

annotations.label: Classical vector analysis example
annotations.note: No worked example of computing flux of a curl over a paraboloid/spherical cap appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Standard domain (Whitney)
annotations.note: Whitney's notion of a `standard domain' (bounded open set whose boundary has zero (n−1)-measure outside a finite union of smooth pieces) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Whitney's Stokes theorem for standard domains
annotations.note: Whitney's Stokes theorem for rough (standard) domains is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classical Stokes' theorem (curl theorem)

anchor.snippet: The classical Stokes' theorem relates the surface integral of the curl of a vector field

annotations.note: There is no `curl` operator or surface-integral=line-integral curl theorem in Mathlib (no files defining curl in the vector-calculus sense).

annotations.label: Stokes' theorem in components (P, Q, R)
annotations.note: The component-wise (P dx + Q dy + R dz) Kelvin–Stokes identity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Green's theorem as special case

anchor.snippet: Green's theorem is immediately recognizable as the third integrand

annotations.note: Green's theorem on rectangles in ℝ² is exactly `integral2_divergence_prod_of_hasFDerivAt` (and its `off_countable` variant).

annotations.label: Maxwell's equations via Stokes' theorem

anchor.snippet: Two of the four Maxwell equations involve curls of 3-D vector fields

annotations.note: Maxwell's equations are not present in Mathlib (no matches for `Maxwell`).

annotations.label: Divergence theorem as special case
annotations.note: The divergence theorem on boxes in ℝⁿ⁺¹ with countable exceptional set is `integral_divergence_of_hasFDerivAt_off_countable`.

annotations.label: Volume integral of gradient of a scalar field

anchor.section: Volume integral of gradient of scalar field

annotations.note: The identity ∫_Ω ∇ψ dV = ∮_∂Ω ψ n dS is not formalized in Mathlib.
